summary:
The facial artery is palpable along the anteroinferior border of the masseter due to its course becoming superficial there. Unlike other arteries like the masseteric or superficial temporal, it uniquely follows this specific path and remains clinically accessible at this site. 

Note: This summary focuses only on direct clinical relevance as requested by user instructions; no extraneous details are included.

The facial artery is palpably located along the anteroinferior border of the masseter because it becomes superficial and travels parallel to this region. It differs from the masseteric and superficial temporal arteries, which do not follow this route or remain detectable at this location.
